# Architecture

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-05 -->

## Overview

`rich-text-renderer.rb` is a Ruby gem that serializes Contentful's `RichText` field type into HTML (or any string format the consumer defines). It is a stateless rendering library — it takes a raw RichText document (a nested hash/JSON structure from the Contentful API) and walks the node tree, delegating each node type to a configurable renderer class. It has no runtime dependencies on the Contentful SDK and no network I/O.

## System Context

This library is an optional companion to `contentful.rb` (the Contentful Delivery API Ruby SDK). Consumers fetch a RichText field from the Contentful API using the SDK, then pass the resulting document hash to this gem for rendering.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Ruby Application] -->|fetch entry| B[contentful.rb / CDA SDK]
    B -->|returns RichText document hash| A
    A -->|renderer.render(document)| C[rich-text-renderer.rb]
    C -->|returns HTML string| A
    A -->|embed in view| D[ERB / Haml / API response]
```

The gem mirrors this pattern across the Contentful SDK ecosystem: analogous renderers exist for JavaScript (`@contentful/rich-text-html-renderer`), Python, PHP, Swift, and Java. All are optional — the core SDKs return raw structured data; rendering is the application's responsibility.

## Internal Structure

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `lib/rich_text_renderer.rb` | Entry point — requires `Renderer` and `Version` |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/renderer.rb` | Top-level `Renderer` class; holds the `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` hash mapping node type strings to renderer classes; accepts custom overrides at initialization |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/base_node_renderer.rb` | Base class for all renderers; implements `find_renderer(node)` — the core dispatch mechanism |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/null_renderer.rb` | Catch-all for unmapped node types; raises a descriptive error by default |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/document_renderers/` | `DocumentRenderer` — renders the root `document` node by iterating top-level content blocks |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/block_renderers/` | One class per block node type (paragraphs, headings, lists, tables, embedded entries/assets, hyperlinks, HR); all extend `BaseBlockRenderer` |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/text_renderers/` | One class per inline mark type (bold, italic, underline, code, superscript, subscript); `TextRenderer` handles text nodes with mark stacking; all extend `BaseInlineRenderer` |
| `lib/rich_text_renderer/version.rb` | Gem version constant (`RichTextRenderer::VERSION`) |
| `spec/` | RSpec tests mirroring `lib/` structure |

## Data Flow

A RichText document is a recursive JSON tree. Each node has a `nodeType` string, a `content` array of child nodes, and a `data` hash for node-specific metadata (e.g., `uri` for hyperlinks, `target` for embedded entries).

```
renderer.render(document)
  → DocumentRenderer#render(document)
    → iterates document['content']
      → BaseNodeRenderer#find_renderer(node)  — looks up node['nodeType'] in @mappings
        → NodeRenderer#render(node)
          → renders tag + render_content(node)  — recurses into node['content']
            → … (recursive for nested blocks)
          → TextRenderer#render(node)  — terminal; applies marks in order via inline renderers
```

Key behavioral notes:
- `nil` key in `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` maps to `NullRenderer`, which raises on unknown types. Consumers can replace this with a silent renderer.
- The `Renderer` merges consumer-supplied mappings over `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` at init — full override granularity, one node type at a time.
- `TextRenderer` deep-clones the node before processing to avoid mutating the input document.
- `TextRenderer` HTML-escapes the raw text value via `CGI.escapeHTML` before applying marks — XSS protection is at this layer.
- Asset renderers (`AssetHyperlinkRenderer`, `AssetBlockRenderer`) use duck typing (`asset.class.ancestors`) rather than `is_a?` checks to avoid a hard dependency on the Contentful SDK's `Asset` class.

## Domain Concepts

**RichText document** — A structured tree of nodes returned by the Contentful API for any `RichText` field. The root node always has `nodeType: "document"`. Not to be confused with Markdown or HTML — it is Contentful's own AST format.

**Node types** — Every node has a `nodeType` string (e.g., `"paragraph"`, `"heading-1"`, `"embedded-entry-block"`, `"text"`). The renderer dispatches on this string. The full set of supported types is the keys of `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` in `renderer.rb`.

**Marks** — Inline text formatting applied to text nodes. A `text` node has a `marks` array where each mark has a `type` string (e.g., `"bold"`, `"italic"`). Marks are applied iteratively by `TextRenderer`, innermost first.

**Embedded entries** — `embedded-entry-block` nodes carry a `data.target` that is a Contentful entry object (SDK model or raw hash). The default `EntryBlockRenderer` just calls `.inspect` on it — **this is intentionally a stub**. Every consumer should override it.

**Embedded assets** — `embedded-asset-block` and `asset-hyperlink` nodes. `AssetBlockRenderer` renders images as `<img>` tags and falls back to `<a>` for other file types.

## Key Dependencies

| Dependency | Why it's here | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `rubocop ~> 1.62.1` | Linting — enforces code style; config in `.rubocop.yml` / `.rubocop_todo.yml` | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `rspec ~> 3` | Test framework | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `rake ~> 13.1.0` | Build tasks (`spec`, `rubocop`, `rspec_rubocop`, `release`) | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `simplecov` | Test coverage reports | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `guard` + `guard-rspec` / `guard-rubocop` | File-watch dev loop | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `yard` | API documentation generation | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |
| `rubygems-tasks` | Adds `gem:build`, `gem:release` rake tasks | `rich_text_renderer.gemspec` |

**Runtime dependencies:** none. The gem has zero runtime gem dependencies — it uses only Ruby stdlib (`CGI`, `Marshal`).

## Configuration

No environment variables or feature flags. Configuration is entirely at instantiation time:

| Parameter | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `Renderer.new(mappings = {})` | Override any or all node-type-to-renderer mappings | `DEFAULT_MAPPINGS` (full set of HTML renderers) |
| `nil` key in mappings | Controls behavior for unknown/unmapped node types | `NullRenderer` (raises) |

## Operational Knowledge

### Deployment

This is a Ruby gem distributed via RubyGems. Releases are manual:

1. Bump version in `lib/rich_text_renderer/version.rb`
2. Update `CHANGELOG.md`
3. Run `bundle exec rake release` — builds the gem, creates a git tag, pushes to RubyGems
4. RubyGems credentials and OTP are in the `CF-Ruby-Chapter` and `Ecosystems` vaults in 1Password
5. Tags in this repo are NOT associated with GitHub Releases — only git tags

Owned by Bhushan Lodha (contractor). See the [Ruby SDK Runbook](https://contentful.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ECO/pages/5499847157) for the full release procedure.

### Failure Modes

- **Unknown node type at render time** — `NullRenderer` raises `"No renderer defined for '...' nodes"`. Consumer must override the `nil` mapping to silence or must add a custom renderer for the node type.
- **`embedded-entry-block` without override** — `EntryBlockRenderer` renders the raw Ruby `.inspect` string wrapped in a `<div>`. This is almost certainly not what consumers want in production.
- **Asset node with non-SDK target** — `AssetHyperlinkRenderer` checks for both SDK asset objects and raw hashes; raises `"Node target is not an asset"` if neither matches.
- **Nil node in content array** — `find_renderer` handles `nil` nodes explicitly; dispatches to the `nil` mapping (NullRenderer by default).

### Monitoring

Service tier 4 (best-effort). CI alerts route to `#sdk-bots` (configured in `catalog-info.yaml`). No application-level monitoring — this is a library with no runtime process.

### Incident Playbook

[NEEDS TEAM INPUT] — As a library, incidents are consumer-side. Escalate via `#sdk-bots` or the DX team (team-developer-experience).

## Integration Points

### Upstream (this repo consumes)

- **Contentful Delivery API** (via `contentful.rb`) — source of RichText document data. This gem is SDK-agnostic; it works with any Ruby hash matching the RichText node schema.

### Downstream (consumes this repo)

- Any Ruby application using Contentful's RichText field type that needs HTML or custom rendering — typically Rails/Sinatra apps, static site generators, API gateways, etc.
- Published to [RubyGems](https://rubygems.org/gems/rich_text_renderer) as `rich_text_renderer`.
